steps.title: Find the main verb
steps.content: The sentence already has the main verb "might yield." The blank modifies "a cultural studies analysis" — describing the analysis. Adding another conjugated verb ("would focus," "would be focusing," "focuses") would create two main verbs without a conjunction, which is ungrammatical.

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
The participle focusing works as a modifier ("a cultural studies analysis focusing on the intersection... might yield..."). It modifies the noun phrase without competing with "might yield" as the main verb.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
